label: AppleScript • liveProof Finder create-if-missing
query: In Finder, create a folder named 'Osaurus Eval Scratch Folder' inside the temporary items folder if it doesn't already exist, then report whether the folder now exists (true or false).
notes: Ground-truth lane for the A3 find-or-create rule against the REAL executor and a REAL app (Finder). The target lives in `path to temporary items` so the side effect is confined to a system-managed scratch location the OS purges. The task must be idempotent: on a rerun the folder already exists and a blind `make new folder` errors, so only a genuine create-if-missing script passes both the first and every subsequent run. The read-back must capture `true`. Needs Finder Automation permission; run locally, permission-gated. Known capability gap at authoring time: the 16B addresses Finder folders by POSIX-path string (`exists folder "/tmp/…"`), which Finder rejects, then reports completion anyway — an honest FAIL row on the scoreboard until the model/knowledge layer closes it. Skips when no AppleScript model is installed.

rubric: The AppleScript creates the folder 'Osaurus Eval Scratch Folder' in the temporary items folder while handling the case where it already exists (checks `exists` or catches the duplicate error), rather than unconditionally creating it.
The script reports whether the folder exists after the operation, and the reported value is true.
